--- SOURCE 1: Partners - Sustainable Finance Observatory ---
URL: https://sustainablefinanceobservatory.org/partners/

SUMMARY:
Partnerships. We partner with a range of actors in the sustainable finance sector, from governments to financial institutions to investor coalitions.

# Partners

## Partnerships

We partner with a range of actors in the sustainable finance sector, from governments to financial institutions to investor coalitions. Find our key project and research partners below:

### About us

UNDP works in 170 countries and territories, helping to eradicate poverty, reduce inequalities and exclusion, and build resilience so countries can sustain progress.

Image

![Image 2: about-us.jpg](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/styles/max_650x650/public/2024-04/about-us.jpg?itok=uzald1ko)

### Our team

Meet UNDP’s Sustainable Finance Leadership Team.

Image

![Image 3: Group photo](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/styles/max_650x650/public/2024-09/Group%20photo%204.png?itok=DsUCmD2L)

### Our partners

UNDP collaborates with partners and networks that champion systemic reforms, and brings an SDG financing focus to its partnerships with international and development finance institutions, business leaders, faith-based finance partners, and other private-sector partners.

Image

![Image 4: elephants-playing-in-the-kruger-2023-11-27-05-19-02-utc](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/styles/max_650x650/public/2024-07/elephants-playing-in-the-kruger-2023-11-27-05-19-02-utc.jpg?itok=oMdnW8fk)

Services
--------

UNDP collaborates with governments, investors, and businesses to transform public and private finance, benefiting both people and the planet. Through the Sustainable Finance Hub, we partner globally to help create a financial system that supports sustainable development and achieves the SDGs.

[Image ![Image 15: saving-bank-1 1.svg ](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/2024-01/saving-bank-1%201.svg) ### Public Finance for the SDGs Aligning public finance, budgets, tax and debt, with the SDGs Learn more](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/our-services/public-finance)

[Image ![Image 16: accounting-coins-bill 1.svg ](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/2024-01/accounting-coins-bill%201.svg) ### Private Finance for the SDGs Leveraging and aligning private capital with the SDGs Learn more](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/our-services/private-finance)

[Image ![Image 17: saving-bank-international 1.svg ](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/2024-01/saving-bank-international%201.svg) ### Integrated National Financing Frameworks Providing a framework for financing national sustainable development priorities and the SDGs Learn more](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/our-services/INFF)

[Image ![Image 18: e-learning-monitor 1.svg ](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/sites/default/files/2024-01/e-learning-monitor%201.svg) ### SDG Finance Academy Building capacity for sustainable finance across the globe Learn more](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/our-services/sdg-finance-academy)

E-Learning
----------

Start your sustainable finance learning journey with our open-access courses.

[Learn more](https://sdgfinance.undp.org/our-services/sdg-finance-academy)

--- SOURCE 5: Partnering for Impact - International Finance Corporation ---
URL: https://www.ifc.org/en/what-we-do/sector-expertise/sustainability/partnering-for-impact

SUMMARY:
IFC partners with the United Nations Sustainable Stock Exchanges Initiative (UN SSE), a global platform promoting exchanges to enhance performance on ESG

FULL CONTENT:
![IFC logo](/content/dam/ifc/corporate/logo/IFC-Logo.svg "IFC logo")

IFC is the largest global development institution focused on the private sector in developing countries. By encouraging growth, IFC advances economic development, creates jobs and improves the lives of people.

IFC advances economic development and improves the lives of people by encouraging the growth of the private sector in developing countries. We achieve this by investing in impactful projects, mobilizing other investors, and sharing expertise.

As the largest global development institution focused on the private sector, IFC operates in more than 100 countries around the globe. We apply lessons learned in one region to solve problems in another.

Our impact improves lives. IFC brings a clear set of advantages to the world’s most challenging markets. Including a history of innovation, a global mandate, and a determination to achieve measurable development impact.

Owned by 186 member countries and consistently rated AAA/Aaa. IFC aims to achieve our mission of promoting development by providing debt and equity to the private sector, through a range of benchmark and bespoke products.

![IFC logo](/content/dam/ifc/corporate/logo/IFC-Logo.svg "IFC logo")
![](https://s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/wbcollab/banner-img?qlt=90&fmt=webp "Print")

# Partnering for Impact

Environmental, social, and governance (ESG) is at the core of the sustainable, long-term success of companies and private investment.

IFC’s ESG approach is governed by its [Sustainability Framework](https://www.ifc.org/en/what-we-do/sector-expertise/sustainability/policies-and-standards#sustainabilityframework)- comprised of the [Sustainability Policy](https://www.ifc.org/en/insights-reports/2012/publications-policy-sustainability-2012), [Performance Standards,](https://www.ifc.org/en/insights-reports/2012/ifc-performance-standards) and [Access to Information Policy](https://www.ifc.org/en/insights-reports/2012/ifc-access-to-information-policy)- and its [Corporate Governance methodology.](https://www.ifc.org/en/what-we-do/sector-expertise/corporate-governance)

Through its ESG advisory services, IFC supports corporate clients, regulators, and other market intermediaries to integrate our standards, and good international industry practices on ESG, into their business practices, guidelines, codes, policies, or regulations.

IFC’s ESG advisory services programs are conducted in partnership with:

global networks to amplify dissemination of good international industry practices.

development partners to scale up resources and harness external expertise and influence.

![](https://s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/wbcollab/IFCCommunityLearningEventDay1-237?qlt=90&fmt=webp)
![](https://s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/wbcollab/IESG-logo-primary?qlt=90&fmt=webp)

[SECO](https://www.seco.admin.ch/seco/en/home.html) is a programmatic partner, supporting global initiatives as well as country level initiatives in multiple regions. [Learn more about the IFC-SECO Integrated ESG Program](/content/dam/ifc/doc/2025/IFC-SECO-Driving-ESG-Impact.pdf).

## Global Networks

## Community of Learning

The Community of Learning, initiated by IFC in 2007, fosters global collaboration among financial institutions and corporations to implement environmental and social standards. This forum facilitates knowledge exchange, case study learning, and dialogue among IFC Performance Standards practitioners. Participants include representatives and members from [Equator Principles](https://equator-principles.com/) Financial Institutions, Development Finance Institutions, Export Credit Agencies, and IFC clients.

## [UN Sustainable Stock Exchanges Initiative](https://sseinitiative.org/)

IFC partners with the [United Nations Sustainable Stock Exchanges Initiative](https://sseinitiative.org/) (UN SSE), a global platform promoting exchanges to enhance performance on ESG issues and sustainable investment, and advance the Sustainable Development Goals. It provides tools, guidance documents and unique research to increase capital access for companies in emerging markets. The initiative focuses on sustainability reporting, women in leadership, SME sustainability practices, and sustainable finance.

## [Sustainable Banking and Finance Network](https://www.sbfnetwork.org/)

Established in 2012, the [Sustainable Banking and Finance Network](https://www.sbfnetwork.org/) (SBFN) is a community of financial sector regulators and industry associations from emerging markets committed to advancing sustainable finance. Facilitated by IFC as secretariat, and supported by the World Bank Group, SBFN provides knowledge, technical assistance and practical support for members to design and implement sustainable finance initiatives at national, regional and global levels.

## [Corporate Governance Development Framework](https://www.ifc.org/en/what-we-do/sector-expertise/corporate-governance/cg-development-framework)

IFC’s Corporate Governance Methodology has been distilled into the [Corporate Governance Development Framework](https://www.ifc.org/en/what-we-do/sector-expertise/corporate-governance/cg-development-framework), providing signatory development finance institutions (DFIs) with a common approach to assess and enhance governance in investee companies. The framework facilitates corporate governance due diligence activities and provides a common reference to engage with client companies and investors in emerging markets on corporate governance issues.

## [The Equator Principles](https://equator-principles.com/)

Established in 2003 and built on the IFC’s Performance Standards, the [Equator Principles](https://equator-principles.com/) provide a common baseline and risk management framework for sustainable investment practices globally. IFC supports the Equator Principles by offering its signatories training, guidance, and advice. In collaboration with the Equator Principles, IFC conducts research on issues related to the IFC Performance Standards and the Equator Principles.

## Our Development Partners

### **Governments**

![](https://s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/wbcollab/1-23?qlt=90&fmt=webp "Untitled design - 1")

[European Union](https://european-union.europa.eu/index_en) through the IFC-EU Partnership to Accelerate Climate Smart, Inclusive Infrastructure Investment in South Asia

![](https://s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/wbcollab/irish-aid-logo-part?qlt=90&fmt=webp "Untitled design - 2")

[Irish Aid, Department of Foreign Affairs](https://www.irishaid.ie/) through the Ireland-IFC Gender Partnership Program

![](https://s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/wbcollab/netherland-logo-part?qlt=90&fmt=webp "Untitled design - 7")

enriched_points.expanded_content: Collaborative partnerships across sectors are essential in sustainable finance, driving innovation and mobilizing resources to tackle global challenges effectively.
Active dialogue between investors and companies fosters transparency and aligns financial strategies with sustainability goals, enhancing long-term value creation in the market.
Non-governmental organizations and governments play a pivotal role in shaping policies and frameworks that facilitate sustainable finance, ensuring accountability and promoting best practices.
Collective efforts among stakeholders, including businesses, governments, and civil society, amplify impact by combining expertise and resources to achieve shared sustainability objectives.
